canonical_definition: ClothoAQA is consistently described across the provided sources as a benchmark or dataset for audio question answering. Its primary purpose is to evaluate a model's audio-language understanding, or as one paper frames it, the "natural language understanding of general audio content" (CalligraphicOCRforChinese Calligraphy Recognition). The dataset is derived from the Clotho dataset, with one source specifying its composition as 1,991 audio samples, "each paired with six crowdsourced questions and corresponding answers." The relationship data confirms its direct use as a measurement instrument for the behavior of audio question answering. One paper refers to it as a "foundational benchmark," and snippets suggest that accuracy (ACC) is a common evaluation metric, with a specific setting or version called "unanimous" also being noted.
papers: Improving Instruct Models for Free: A Study on Partial Adaptation
Audio Flamingo 2: An Audio-Language Model with Long-Audio Understanding and Expert Reasoning Abilities
MATS: An Audio Language Model under Text-only Supervision

definitions.text: An audio question-answering benchmark derived from Clotho for evaluating audio-language understanding.
definitions.paper: Audio Flamingo 2: An Audio-Language Model with Long-Audio Understanding and Expert Reasoning Abilities
